response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack engineer, but the profile does not match the core requirements of this role. C++ experience is minimal and dated — roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) plus an academic thesis project — far short of the required 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. There is no SQL Server or Oracle experience (only MongoDB/MySQL/SQL generally), and no fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain exposure. The primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. Communication skills are clearly strong, but they cannot offset the absence of the mandatory technical and domain qualifications.
strengths: Strong, broad engineering track record with TDD, CI/CD, and craftsmanship practices
Excellent communication evidenced by conference talks, workshops, and courses
Some genuine C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and in a thesis project
concerns: C++ experience is brief (~6 months) and roughly a decade old, not the required 5–10 years
No SQL Server/Oracle and no fixed income or capital-markets/investment-banking domain
No demonstrated Windows-platform C++ development; primary stack is JS/TS and DLang
